name: Machine Chest Press
resistance_profile: Fixed path lets you train the chest hard and safely to near failure.
rom_notes: Allow a comfortable stretch at the chest, press to a controlled (not slammed) lockout.
cues: Shoulder blades set back and down
Press with the chest, not just the arms
execution_steps: Adjust the seat so the handles line up with the middle of your chest.
Set your shoulder blades back and down against the pad and grip the handles.
Press the handles forward until your arms are nearly straight, without slamming the lockout.
Lower under control to a comfortable stretch at the chest and repeat.
good_when: You are a beginner or training alone and want to press hard toward failure safely.
You want a stable, low-fatigue chest option to add volume.
You want to isolate the chest without balancing free weights.
bad_when: You want to develop stabilizer strength and a natural bar path (use free weights).
Only a fixed machine that does not fit your body is available.
